goal: MCP server exposing gad CLI as runtime-agnostic tools — separate track from skills. Operator 2026-04-22 asked about mcp_skills approach. Clarification: MCP is for TOOL EXPOSURE across runtimes (codex, cursor, gemini, opencode can all consume it), NOT for skill enforcement (that's Claude Code Skill()). Scope: MCP server gad-mcp exposing tools (gad_snapshot, gad_state_log, gad_tasks_add, gad_tasks_stamp, gad_decisions_add, gad_handoffs_create, gad_state_set_next_action, gad_phase_show, gad_skill_list, gad_skill_show, etc.). One server, any MCP-speaking runtime discovers + calls. Existing bin/gad-mcp.cjs is the starting point — audit its current tool list, extend to cover the canonical subcommand surface, document in references/mcp-tools.md. Does NOT replace skills — MCP tools are stateless capability calls, skills are structured workflows.
